英國英格蘭貝德福爾瑟利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane de Paston於1387年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可柏斯頓時，她的父親Clement de Paston II32歲，母親Beatrice de Somerton33歲。她與Thomas Hervey Sr結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1488年2月1日在她的家鄉享年101歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可柏斯頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane de Paston于1387年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可柏斯頓时，她的父亲Clement de Paston II32岁，母亲Beatrice de Somerton33岁。她与Thomas Hervey Sr结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1488年2月1日在她的家乡享年101岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可柏斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Paston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Norfolk): habitational name, chiefly from Paston in Norfolk, probably derived from an Old English personal name Pæcci (genitive Pæcces) or Old English pæsc(e) (the source of northern English dialect pash ‘puddle’) + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

English (West Midlands): sometimes a variant of Patstone, a habitational name chiefly from the lost Padston(e) in Bromsgrove (Worcestershire), and occasionally from Padson in Inwardleigh (Devon). The Worcestershire placename derives from the Old English personal name Padda (genitive Paddes) + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The etymology of the Devon placename is uncertain due to a lack of medieval forms, though an early spelling suggests the final element is Old English dūn ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane de Paston was born in 1387, in Paston, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Clement de Paston II, was 32 and her mother, Beatrice de Somerton, was 33. She married Thomas Hervey Sr. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died on 1 February 1488, in her hometown, at the age of 101, and was buried in Paston, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane de Paston est née en 1387, à Paston, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Clement de Paston II, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Beatrice de Somerton, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Hervey Sr. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée le 1 février 1488, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 101 ans, et a été enterrée à Paston, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane de Paston 1387 in Paston, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Clement de Paston II 32 und ihre Mutter Beatrice de Somerton 33. Sie hat Thomas Hervey Sr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb am 1. Februar 1488 im Alter von 101 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Paston, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane de Paston è nata il nel 1387 nel Paston, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Clement de Paston II, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Beatrice de Somerton, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Hervey Sr. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì il 1 febbraio 1488, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 101 anni, e fu sepolta a Paston, Norfolk, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane de Paston(이)가 1387년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 파스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Clement de Paston II(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrice de Somerton(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 Thomas Hervey Sr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1488년 2월 1일 그녀의 고향에서 101세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 파스턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane de Paston родилась в 1387 г. в местности пастон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Clement de Paston II было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrice de Somerton, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Hervey Sr. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла 1 февраля 1488 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 101 год и была похоронена в местности пастон, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane de Paston nació en 1387, en Paston, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Clement de Paston II, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Beatrice de Somerton, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Thomas Hervey Sr. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió el 1 de febrero de 1488, en su pueblo, a la edad de 101 años, y fue sepultada en Paston, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
